title: Accumulator-passing foldl equals reverse-append
informal_statement: For any list l and accumulator acc of naturals, folding l with the prepend operation (fun a x => x :: a) starting from acc equals l.reverse ++ acc.
informal_proof_idea: Induction on l. Base case nil: both sides reduce to acc by simp. Successor case cons x xs with ih: simp [ih] closes the goal directly, bridging the accumulator shift (acc vs x :: acc) via the fold/append simp normal form -- despite acc being fixed rather than generalized before the induction, simp's rewriting is strong enough here that the classic 'induction without generalizing the accumulator' failure mode does not manifest.
formal_statement_pp: theorem foldl_cons_eq_reverse_append (l acc : List ℕ) : l.foldl (fun a x => x :: a) acc = l.reverse ++ acc

review.review_basis: repo self-review; kernel-verified through the tracked proof-search loop

notes: Proof authority: Lean kernel via the tracked proof-search loop (episode 0ab12a3b, problem_version 204fecc3-8ba6-4fac-bf30-03dec9b20ad4). Originally attempted as a negative example for the 'induction without generalizing an auxiliary variable' hazard queued in packets/negative/induction/QUEUE.md: the intended natural-but-wrong attempt (induction l without generalizing acc, then simp [ih]) actually succeeded (steps 1-2 were transport/syntax failures unrelated to the intended lesson -- raw_lean_block mis-parsed a multi-line `induction ... with` block, then a retry omitted the now-necessary `intro l acc`; step 3, `intro l acc; induction l with | nil => simp | cons x xs ih => simp [ih]`, reached kernel_verified). Per this domain's own queue policy, a hypothesis that turns out provable is not a valid negative example -- authored as a positive packet instead; the QUEUE.md candidate is corrected to suggest a weaker closing tactic for anyone re-attempting the negative-example version of this trap. domain: algebra is intentional, matching every other packet filed under the `induction` folder, since `induction` is not a valid `domain` enum value in schema/packet.schema.json.
